stub: He is a Labour MP who comes from a white middle class background.
constituency: He is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: He tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of his party, even when that conflicts with his own beliefs.
substantive: He supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, he regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “I can only change society if I am re-elected. So, I always make sure that I listen to voters’ most important concerns.”
text: He is a Labour MP who comes from a white middle class background. He is from a constituency that is not your constituency. He tends to vote in accordance with the instructions of his party, even when that conflicts with his own beliefs. He supports increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, he regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, he said: “I can only change society if I am re-elected. So, I always make sure that I listen to voters’ most important concerns.”
